source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Hint: Please answer the question requiring an integer answer and provide the final value, e.g., 1, 2, 3, at the end.
Question: Consider a closed-loop control system with unity negative feedback and \( KG(s) \) in the forward path, where the gain \( K = 2 \). The complete Nyquist plot of the transfer function \( G(s) \) is shown in the figure. Note that the Nyquist contour has been chosen to have the clockwise sense. Assume \( G(s) \) has no poles on the closed right-half of the complex plane. The number of poles of the closed-loop transfer function in the closed right-half of the complex plane is ______.
extraction_notes: image field exists but is an in-memory PIL image placeholder, not a path/URL/filename

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the complete Nyquist plot is shown in the figure
solving depends on visual properties of the Nyquist plot such as encirclements/location relative to the critical point
multi_step: requires combining the given gain K=2 with the plotted G(s), effectively reasoning about KG(s) or the equivalent critical point
requires applying the Nyquist criterion using the stated clockwise contour convention
requires using the assumption that G(s) has no poles on the closed right-half plane to relate encirclements to the number of closed-loop right-half-plane poles
this is image-text joint reasoning rather than direct lookup from text alone
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: 2
the target quantity is well-defined: number of poles of the closed-loop transfer function in the closed right-half plane
the problem provides an explicit assumption about open-loop pole location, which supports rule-based checking once encirclements are read from the plot
registry_passed=true and the image asset exists, so the necessary artifact for verification is present
